input: Recommend formulation strategy for a BCS Class II compound (low solubility, high permeability). MW 420, LogP 4.5, melting point 185°C.
Formulate a BCS Class I compound (high solubility, high permeability) as an immediate-release oral tablet.

expected_details.approach: conventional IR tablet — direct compression or wet granulation

input: Formulation challenge: oral delivery of a 4 kDa peptide drug (currently IV only). What approaches are feasible?
Design a once-daily controlled-release formulation for a drug with t½=4h, therapeutic range 5-15 μg/mL.
Formulate a monoclonal antibody (150 kDa IgG1) for lyophilized presentation. Specify excipients and process parameters.
A compound has aqueous solubility of 2 μg/mL and LogP 5.8. Recommend a nanocrystal approach.

expected_details.technique: wet media milling or high-pressure homogenization
expected_details.advantages: increased dissolution rate via Noyes-Whitney equation

input: Develop a pediatric formulation for a bitter-tasting antibiotic. Target age group: 2-6 years.

expected_details.excipient_safety: avoid ethanol, propylene glycol, parabens per EMA pediatric guideline

input: Formulate a topical NSAID for knee osteoarthritis. Maximize skin penetration to reach joint.
A tablet formulation shows 5% degradation at 3 months (40°C/75% RH). The degradant is an oxidation product. Recommend stabilization strategies.
Formulation considerations for an antibody-drug conjugate (ADC). DAR=4, linker-payload is maleimide-MMAE.
